name: Riaño from Cistierna – circular through Riaño Mountain Regional Park and Mampodre
name_original: Riaño desde Cistierna – circular por Parque Regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre
region_intro: Road cycling around Cistierna is characterized by its mountain scenery, forming part of the Cantabrian Mountains and offering access to the Riaño and Mampodre Mountain Regional Park. The region features a mix of paved roads with low traffic, ideal for cycle tourism, and challenging mountain passes. Cyclists can experience routes that wind alongside the Riaño Reservoir, often referred to as the "Leonese Fjords," and through diverse ecosystems. This varied terrain provides significant elevation gains and visually engaging rides. Best road cycling routes around Cistierna The most popular road cycling route is Riaño from Cistierna – circular through Riaño Mountain Regional Park and Mampodre, a 78.0 miles (125.6 km) trail that takes 6 hours 4 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive climbs through protected mountain landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Río Cea from Cistierna – circular by Riaño, a difficult 58.2 miles (93.6 km) path. This route provides scenic views along the Río Cea and near the Riaño Reservoir. Local road cyclists also love the Cistierna - Leonese Mining Basins Loop, a 74.4 miles (119.8 km) trail leading through the Leonese mining basins, often completed in about 4 hours 56 minutes. Road cycling around Cistierna is defined by mountain scenery, the Cantabrian Mountains, and the picturesque Riaño Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging mountain passes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 380 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cistierna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Cistierna?
A: The region around Cistierna is known for its stunning mountain scenery, forming part of the Cantabrian Mountains. You can expect a mix of paved roads with low traffic, ideal for classic cycle tourism, and challenging mountain passes that offer significant elevation gains. Routes often wind alongside impressive water bodies like the Riaño Reservoir, providing diverse and visually engaging rides.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders in Cistierna?
A: Yes, Cistierna offers several challenging routes suitable for experienced road cyclists seeking demanding climbs and descents. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042276277/riano-from-cistierna-circular-through-riano-mountain-regional-park-and-mampodre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riaño from Cistierna – circular through Riaño Mountain Regional Park and Mampodre&lt;/a&gt; route is a difficult 125.6 km trail with extensive climbs through protected mountain landscapes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042288573/rio-cea-from-cistierna-circular-by-riano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Cea from Cistierna – circular by Riaño&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 93.6 km path with considerable elevation.

Q: Are there any moderate or easier road cycling options around Cistierna?
A: While many routes feature mountain terrain, there are moderate options available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046787875/cistierna-leonese-mining-basins-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cistierna - Leonese Mining Basins Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 119.8 km trail that takes you through the Leonese mining basins. Another moderate choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3990667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cistierna – Boñar loop from Cistierna&lt;/a&gt;, a 55.3 km route with a more manageable elevation gain.

Q: What scenic viewpoints or natural features can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes around Cistierna offer breathtaking views, especially of the Riaño Reservoir, often called the “Leonese Fjords.” You can enjoy sights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6308896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Riaño Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5553308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Pico Gilbo, Pico Yordas, and Riaño Reservoir from Mirador Las Breñas Dondes Macho&lt;/a&gt;. The region's diverse ecosystems, including oaks, pines, and beeches, also contribute to the visually engaging rides.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical landmarks to explore while road cycling near Cistierna?
A: Yes, Cistierna has a rich history. You can incorporate cultural stops into your routes, such as visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443521" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hórreo of Salio - Riaño&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5475673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente del Mercadillo&lt;/a&gt;. The town itself has an ethnographic museum and a Museum of the Railway, and serves as a starting point to explore nearby historical villages and castles.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Cistierna?
A: The region is generally best for road cycling during the warmer months, from late spring through early autumn. This period offers more stable weather conditions and allows for full enjoyment of the mountain passes and scenic routes. Winter conditions can be challenging due to snow and colder temperatures in the higher elevations of the Cantabrian Mountains.

Q: Are there any loop routes available for road cycling around Cistierna?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Cistierna are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042276277/riano-from-cistierna-circular-through-riano-mountain-regional-park-and-mampodre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riaño from Cistierna – circular through Riaño Mountain Regional Park and Mampodre&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042288573/rio-cea-from-cistierna-circular-by-riano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Cea from Cistierna – circular by Riaño&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046787875/cistierna-leonese-mining-basins-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cistierna - Leonese Mining Basins Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Cistierna?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the stunning mountain scenery, the dramatic backdrop of the Cantabrian Mountains, and the picturesque views of the Riaño Reservoir. The mix of challenging climbs and quiet paved roads with low traffic is also frequently highlighted as a major draw.

Q: Are there opportunities to see wildlife while cycling in the Cistierna region?
A: The diverse ecosystems around Cistierna are home to rich flora and fauna. While cycling, you might encounter various species in their natural habitat, including oaks, pines, and beeches. The region is also known for larger wildlife such as wolves, foxes, and even brown bears, particularly within the Riaño and Mampodre Mountain Regional Park, offering an immersive natural experience.

Q: What other outdoor activities are available in Cistierna besides road cycling?
A: Beyond road cycling, Cistierna is a paradise for nature lovers. You can explore numerous hiking and walking trails, including sections of the 'Camino Olvidado.' There's also a via ferrata for climbing enthusiasts, opportunities for cave exploration, and river activities on the Esla River. These activities complement a cycling trip, offering a well-rounded outdoor experience.

Q: Is there good parking available for cyclists in Cistierna?
A: Cistierna serves as a convenient starting point for many routes, and as a town, it generally offers parking options. Cyclists can typically find parking within the town or at designated areas near popular trailheads, making it easy to access the cycling routes.

Possibly the best point of departure and arrival for a road bike route, with destinations to the ports of El Pando, San Glorio, Pandetrave, Panderrueda and Tarna. Many restaurants, bars, cafes and a small supermarket to eat something. There are also hotels and a campsite. I recommend going on the famous swing, but only if there are few people in the queue.
Great views and quiet, flat places to spend the night. (Do not have water)

An artificial reservoir to harness the power of the waters of the Esla River, the Porma Reservoir is the third largest reservoir in the province of León. An incredible cycling route with spectacular views of the surrounding mountains.

waypoints.name: View of the Riaño Reservoir and Bridge
